channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: England vs India T20 delayed after visitors get stuck in traffic LIVE!
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football

item.title: Klopp agrees deal in principle to manage Germany
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Jurgen Klopp is on the verge of becoming Germany's new head coach after reaching an agreement in principle with the German Football Association, according to Sky in Germany.

item.title: Much-changed Ireland beat Japan in Nations Championship as debutants impress
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: A much-changed Ireland side overcame Japan 36-20 to make it two wins from two in the Nations Championship.

item.title: Hewett and Reid win seventh Wimbledon wheelchair doubles title
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Britain's Alfie Hewett and Gordon Reid won their seventh wheelchair doubles title together at Wimbledon on Saturday.

item.title: Morning session highlights: India take four wickets to stay on top of England
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Highlights of the morning session on day two of the Women's Test between England and India.

item.description: Former Vice President Atiku Abubakar has described the rescue of the abducted schoolchildren and teachers from Oyo State as The post Atiku welcomes rescue of abducted schoolchildren, teachers in Oyo appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Gov Okpebholo scores voter turnout high as observers hail peaceful Edo LG polls
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: From Tony Osauzo, Benin Edo State Governor, Senator Monday Okpebholo, has described voter turnout in yesterday’s Local Government Council elections in the State as encouraging, while accredited election observers said voting was peaceful and orderly across the state. The governor spoke after c

item.title: FUGUS Approves Promotion Of 12 Professors, 12 Readers
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: In furtherance of its commitment to strengthening academic excellence, scholarship, and institutional development, the Governing Council of Federal University Gusau (FUGUS) has approved the promotion of twelve Academic Staff to the position of Professor and twelve others to the rank of Reader. In a

item.title: First Lady urges Nigerians to register for NIN, calls for greater investment in people on World Population Day
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: From Juliana Taiwo-Obalonye, Abuja First Lady Oluremi Tinubu has called on Nigerians to register for the National Identity Number (NIN) and urged increased investment in the country’s people as part of global observance of World Population Day 2026. In a message released on Saturday, July 11, Mrs. T

item.title: ICE are heavily armed killers. They’re also huge losers
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: Donald Trump's Homeland Security regime has been at the center of two critical stories in the past two weeks. In the first, federal agents shot and killed a man and quickly got to work justifying the use of force under the flimsiest of pretenses. In the other, it made house calls to people who said

item.title: Nigerian stocks gain 6.35% in one week, as bulls takeover
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: The Nigerian equities market held nearly steady on Friday, July 10, 2026, as the benchmark NGX All-Share Index depreciated marginally by 0.065% to close at 243,798.76 points, virtually unchanged from Thursday's close of 243,958.73 points. The post Nigerian stocks gain 6.35% in one week, as bulls tak

item.title: African Swine Fever hits Plateau, 1,000 pigs killed, farms shut
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: From Jude Owuamanam, Jos Pig farmers in Plateau State have have been forced to shut their farms following an outbreak of African Swine Fever (ASF) in the state. The farmers said thousands of pigs have died with losses running into billions of naira. Chief Executive Officer of Topnotch Pig Farms and

item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: US officials and diplomats and their families began reporting a mysterious illness a decade ago.

item.title: Niger Police drag motorists to court as 82 vehicles fall in tinted glass, number plate crackdown
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Niger State Police Command has prosecuted several motorists after intercepting 82 vehicles during an ongoing statewide enforcement against unregistered vehicles, defaced number plates and illegal tinted glass. The crackdown, which began on June 20, 2026, is part of the Inspector-General of Polic

item.title: Troops, local hunters destroy Boko Haram hideouts after Miringa attack in Borno
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Troops of Operation HADIN KAI have carried out a clearance operation across suspected Boko Haram and Islamic State West Africa Province, ISWAP, hideouts in Biu Local Government Area of Borno State following the recent attack on Miringa community. The development was disclosed in a report by Zagazola

item.title: Indian tourists among 15 killed as speedboat capsizes in Vietnam
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Local media say 21 people were saved after the boat overturned in rough seas near an island in south Vietnam.

item.description: The Sultan of Sokoto and President-General of the Nigerian Supreme Council for Islamic Affairs, Muhammad Sa'ad Abubakar III, has described the rescue of the 44 persons in the old Oyo National Park as a renewed source of hope for Nigeria in the fight against insecurity. item.title: SOKAPU Offers Peace, Seeks End To Southern Kaduna Leadership Crisis

item.description: The Southern Kaduna Peoples’ Union (SOKAPU) has expressed its readiness to end the protracted leadership crisis rocking the region, declaring its willingness to embrace dialogue and pursue an out-of-court settlement with other Southern Kaduna groups in the interest of peace and unity. item.title: Charging the ANC for Afrophobia and xenophobia, By Ahmed Aminu-Ramatu Yusuf

item.description: We compelled our governments to fund ANC’s activities within and outside Nigeria. Scholarships were provided for Black South African students to study in our universities and polytechnics… We, students, ensured that Nigeria became a home away from home for South African students by personally

item.title: Can Zverev deny unstoppable Sinner from defending Wimbledon title?
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Jannik Sinner will head into his second Wimbledon final on Sunday as big favourite to retain the title having beaten Alexander Zverev in their last nine meetings.

T

item.title: Paris landmarks to close early as France swelters under third heatwave since May
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: The Eiffel Tower and other Paris landmarks, including the Louvre and the Musée d'Orsay, will close early over the weekend as France is hit by its third heatwave since May. Across the country, many towns have also cancelled their fireworks displays for Bastille Day due to increased fires and dry cond

item.title: From China’s Shock 2.0 to China Opportunity 2.0: What Africa and Nigeria should learn from Premier Li Qiang’s Summer Davos message
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: By Leslie Osigbemhe   When Chinese Premier Li Qiang stood before world leaders at the 2026 Summer Davos Forum in Dalian, China, he did much more than defend China’s economic model. He sought to redefine one of the most contentious debates in the global economy. The event, 17th Annual Meet

item.title: ‘Day-to-day dread’ haunts frustrated job seekers in era of low hiring. When will it end?
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Finding a job was easy a few years ago. Now, Americans are worried about their job prospects, even though the unemployment rate is historically low.

Reacting to the rescue of the victims, a niece of the late Oyedokun, identifie

item.title: 'Greed' and loopholes: How cricket's $55 million development fund gets gamed
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: The International Cricket Council distributes tens of millions of dollars per year in development funding through a system that insiders say is loosely monitored and easily manipulated, a Play the Game investigation has found in partnership with FRANCE 24.

item.description: Roleplaying games are often defined by excess. Storylines that span dozens of hours, side quests so big they could be their own game, massive worlds that require complex maps to explore, and casts so big you start forgetting character names. That's part of what makes these games feel like epic adven

item.title: Regular water is no longer enough. Welcome to the corporate gold rush around ‘functional hydration.’
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Social media and GLP-1s are spurring increased interest in hydration products, and companies are responding with things like electrolyte mixes and “hydrating hot chocolate.”

item.dc:creator: SDK Celeb
item.description: There are friendships you lose and feel sad about… and there are friendships you lose and quietly thank God. Not because you are heartless, but because you finally realise how much unnecessary weight you were carrying in the name of friendship...Some people stay in your life without adding a single

item.dc:creator: The Guardian
item.description: Vigilantes also took part in the fight that raged all night and the following morning, residents sayNigerian soldiers killed more than 300 members of kidnapping and cattle bandit gangs in the north-western state of Zamfara this week, according to a government official.Government troops targeted the

item.title: From Arsenal jeers to Swiss tears: Xhaka's redemption
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Granit Xhaka has battled back from being booed by his own fans at Arsenal to being one of the most influential players for club and country as he looks to help Switzerland make history at the World Cup.

item.description: When Rayda launched in 2023, it wanted to help businesses keep track of their assets. Three years later, it wants to manage everything that happens between an employee joining a company and leaving it.

item.title: Sheinbaum takes on cartels, Trump and the legacy of 1968
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Claudia Sheinbaum is trying to govern on her own terms — while dealing with Trump, cartels and AMLO's shadow.

item.title: Gov Eno sympathises with flood victims, cautions against blockage of drains
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: As residents of Uyo, Akwa Ibom State continue to count their losses over the flooding incident of Thursday, Governor Umo Eno has expressed his sympathy for victims. While describing rainfall as a natural occurrence, he warned residents against dumping refuse into drainage channels, saying that

item.title: 10 Greatest Nigerian Performers In WAFCON History
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: The 2026 Women’s Africa Cup of Nations will get under way in Morocco on Sunday, 26 July, with defending champions Nigeria aiming to capture a record-extending 11th continental title. Over the years, a host of Super Falcons stars have left an indelible mark on Africa’s biggest women&#8217

item.title: Speedboat accident in southern Vietnam kills 15 Indian tourists
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: The Indian embassy says it has set up control rooms to respond to victims' families.

item.title: Atiku lauds rescue of Oyo pupils, teachers
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Former Vice President Atiku Abubakar has lauded the rescue of school children and teachers abducted in Orire area of Oyo State, on May 15, and called on the Federal Government to sustain onslaught against terrorists across the country. The post Atiku lauds rescue of Oyo pupils, teachers appeared fir

item.title: The U.S. stock market is becoming ‘too big to fail’
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Why protracted bear markets may be a thing of the past.
